summary: Large two-level institutional or industrial-style building with a steep stepped brick roof, pale sandstone-like walls with white trim and a surrounding low stone/parapet boundary; includes a tall separate tower column with a stacked cap. Open ground around structure and many narrow horizontal windows.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main volume with stepped sloped roof and an attached tall rectangular tower; low raised foundation/coping runs around perimeter
shape.symmetry: approximately symmetrical along the long axis but with asymmetrical tower placement and stepped roof on one side

elements: long stepped brick pitched roof with visible stair-step edge
two storey pale walls with horizontal bands and repetitive narrow windows
white/quartz corner and banding trim forming piers and window surrounds
separate tall rectangular tower with capped roof
low stone/parapet blocks surrounding building perimeter
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular two-floor shell of sandstone (or sandstone-like) with vertical quartz corner piers and horizontal quartz bands between floors.
Place a steep stepped brick gable roof running along the long axis; use brick blocks with stone slab edging at the ridge and eaves to mimic the shown step detail.
Add narrow horizontal glass windows in repeated rows on both floors aligned within the sandstone bands.
Construct a separate tall rectangular tower (approx 3-4 blocks wide) with sandstone shaft, quartz vertical trim, small square windows, and a layered cap using quartz and stone slabs.
Surround building footprint with a low stonebrick/parapet one-block-high wall and place quartz blocks as foundation coping at base.
Use stone slabs along roof edges and between brick and quartz trim where photos show thin horizontal separators.
uncertainties: Exact internal floor layouts and stair placement unknown (not visible).
Material identification between quartz, smooth sandstone, or white concrete is uncertain; labeled as quartz/sandstone based on color and texture.
Exact footprint dimensions estimated from perspective; depth and width may vary by a few blocks.
Roof is interpreted as a gable with stepped detail; could be a partial hip or custom stepped roof variant.
